note_data.title: Circular measure
keyPoints: **Radian** is the angle subtended at the centre of a circle by an arc equal in length to the radius
**Arc length** s = rОё where r is radius and Оё is angle in radians
**Sector area** A = ВҪrВІОё where Оё is in radians
**Convert degrees to radians**: multiply by ПҖ/180 or use Оё(rad) = ПҖОё(deg)/180
**Convert radians to degrees**: multiply by 180/ПҖ or use Оё(deg) = 180Оё(rad)/ПҖ
**Small angle approximations**: sin Оё вүҲ Оё, tan Оё вүҲ Оё, cos Оё вүҲ 1 - ОёВІ/2 when Оё is small and in radians
**Perimeter of sector** = 2r + rОё where Оё is in radians
For a **complete circle**: circumference = 2ПҖr, area = ПҖrВІ, angle = 2ПҖ radians = 360В°

definitions.definition: The angle subtended at the centre of a circle by an arc whose length is equal to the radius of the circle
The distance along the curved path of a circle between two points, calculated using s = rОё where Оё is in radians
The region of a circle enclosed by two radii and an arc
When Оё is small and measured in radians: sin Оё вүҲ Оё, tan Оё вүҲ Оё, cos Оё вүҲ 1 - ОёВІ/2

howToAnswer.instruction: Show all working steps and ensure angle is in correct units (radians for arc length and area formulas)
Multiply by appropriate conversion factor and show the calculation clearly
Identify which formula to use, substitute values correctly, and calculate the final answer

modelAnswer.question: A sector of a circle has radius 8 cm and arc length 12 cm. Find (a) the angle of the sector in radians, (b) the area of the sector.
modelAnswer.answer:
(a) Using s = rОё, so Оё = s/r = 12/8 = 1.5 radians
(b) Using A = ВҪrВІОё, so A = ВҪ Г— 8ВІ Г— 1.5 = ВҪ Г— 64 Г— 1.5 = 48 cmВІ

commonMistakes: Never use degrees in arc length formula s = rОё вҖ” always convert to radians first
Students often forget to convert between degrees and radians when required
Never use A = ВҪ Г— base Г— height for sector area вҖ” always use A = ВҪrВІОё
Students confuse arc length with chord length вҖ” arc length follows the curve
Never approximate ПҖ as 22/7 unless specifically told вҖ” leave as ПҖ or use calculator value
examinerTips: This topic appears frequently in Paper 1 and often combined with trigonometry
Always check units вҖ” if angle given in degrees, convert to radians for formulas
Draw a clear diagram showing radius, arc length, and angle for full marks
Small angle approximations only work when Оё is in radians and typically Оё < 0.2
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Circle with centre O, radius r, showing sector with angle Оё (in radians), arc length s, and two radii forming the sector boundaries
